Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW (C)
Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW (D)
Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW (E)

name: Ascenders Banded Brunch Bunch Ride
description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Ascenders Brunch Bunch for a chilled Rubber Banded ride around the virtual roads of Zwift.  Each week the course will vary so whether you’re a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, this is a great way to start. If recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy. You’ll be very welcome in the Brunch Bunch


This is a banded group ride and not a race. As long as you keep spinning you will stay with the group. If you’re looking for an easy ride then sit in the group near the Yellow Lead Beacon. However if you’re feeling strong then head to the front and help pull the group along.


Please note this ride is not fenced.


Ascenders Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift <https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift>

Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh <https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh>

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Banded Brunch Bunch Ride (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to [ASC] - Beanie’s Brunch Bunch for a chilled ride around the virtual roads of Zwift. Each week the course will vary so, whether you’re a beginner just starting out on your first group rides, recovering from injury, or just looking for a steady paced ride then come and join us. We aim to provide a safe encouraging ride for you to enjoy.

This is a group ride and not a race so please respect the pace and ride with the group. The fence will be used to aid regrouping and politely remind those out front to ease back to the main group. If you’re a stronger confident rider then ride behind the Lead Beacon and lend a draft for others who may need it.

PS Bring your own Brunch…

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

name: The Herd Thunder Down Under
description:
Join us for a relaxed social ride with The Herd Thunder Down Under and Aussie Herdlings. Let's improve our mindsets and release some stress from your day. We’re stronger together. 

Average pace will be 1.6w/kg to 2.0w/kg.
You’re welcome to sprint, just start behind the leader and regroup after. All riders are welcome to join. We have sweepers to help, if you fall off the pace. Stronger riders are encouraged to help sweep. This is NOT A RACE!
We are a supportive community and our goal is to encourage new riders to join.

Please join us on Discord during the ride, and our community at http://theherd.club/

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 6
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: Run 10K Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the longest running daily led running event on Zwift. This was the event that kickstarted the NoblePro Running Club in support of a world record attempt at consecutive daily10k runs.
The event has a healthy group of leaders who are friendly and chatty and will help you achieve your goal, not matter your experience. Feel free to join in the chat and become addicted like we all are.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 10k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, each day of the week there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: Run 10K Every Day Powered By NoblePro (E)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: PACK🐺 1.5 Beginners Ride
description:
This PACK 1.5 beginner friendly event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience at an average lap pace of around 1.3-1.5w/kg.

On the flat, the pace will be controlled to ensure the group draft effect is kept under tight control with a target of 1.5w/kg on any hills or upslopes. The Leader will text instructions as well as use discord voice chat as communication is the key to a good group ride. Sweepers will also be in operation to assist in helping the group stay together as this is a non-drop ride.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: 6Points Challenges Group Thursday Training Ride
description:
This is a TRAINING event, category D, with a yellow beacon leader. The beacon leader will ride up to a 2.5w/kg pace average wherever possible. The beacon may employ the fence at times.

Please look out for this training ride on Thursdays, every week, at 9.55am UTC in the UK, 4.55am EDT in the US, and at 10.55 CET in Europe. Use #6Points to search for our rides on the Zwift website.

This weekly 6Points Mallorca Thursday training ride is intended to provide a challenge for riders, and is open to all timezones. You should ride as a group, following the instructions of the beacon ride leader(s) for any sprints, intervals and spicy segments, regrouping after each when requested. All our group rides also have a closing minirace, to start from behind the beacon. The fence may be used to control starts for these spicy sections.
About the in-real-life Reverence 6Points Mallorca
The ultra-distance Reverence 6Points Mallorca real life cycling event, with Title sponsor Reverence Hotels, is ridden around Mallorca and raises money for charity, currently supporting Asdica, who work on behalf of disabled children, and Save-the-Med. Now in its third edition, it has already earned a reputation as a fun but tough event that epitomises cycling at its best. 6Points has raised over €100,000 for charities in this way over three years.

6Points Mallorca is a ride around the whole of the beautiful island of Mallorca, covering well over 420 kms, climbing 7500 metres and touching the four compass extremities, as well as the highest and lowest points of Mallorca, over 3 days. See https://6pointschallenges.com/ for more about our in-real-life events. We also run these events in Ibiza / Formentera and Menorca.

Like our Zwift group ride, 6Points Mallorca is NOT a race. It is an opportunity to participate in a challenging ride, with like-minded cyclists, to enjoy Mallorca’s diverse beauty and the friendship and camaraderie of fellow riders. 6Points supports sustainable cycling, whose riders care for the environment and practise courtesy to fellow road users.

We have organized our 6Points Zwift training ride series to suit what we believe is relevant to bike riders’ interests in performance and improvement, bearing in mind the in-real-life 6Points Mallorca challenge which is also ridden in groups with controlled pace.
The 6Points Zwift group ride
As we expect our riders to be able to ride up to 100 miles at or around a 15mph average on our irl (in-real-life) rides, such steady riding members should be comfortable with Zwift Category D. Beacon leaders communicate via Zwift message (always) and/or Discord (sometimes) to vary the pace to allow for sprints, intervals and optionally a mini-race towards the end of the rides, with regrouping after each sprint. Make as much use of drafting as you can, for more effective sprinting and rest intervals.

During the ride, the beacon will announce any sprints, and the ride will finish with a minirace to the end of the ride of between 3kms and 8kms depending on the course. The Beacon will announce this too.

Results (the finishing order, relevant to the minirace which we will all start together) can be seen at the end of the ride on screen, and we will, in common with many other ride series, publish your results on zwiftpower.com too, based on the ride category D.

All bikes and Power-Ups (PU) are allowed, but it is better not to use TT bikes to ensure drafting. Please add 6Points (X) to your Zwift name to allow your ride presence to be recognized, where X is your own ride category. Categories in Zwift are related to your Functional Threshold Power (FTP); use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg to get the W/kg that applies to you.

On this ride series, all riders and categories are welcome, but bear in mind that rides are designed for:

Category D: 2 W/kg to 2.5 W/kg FTP. The beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average or below.

PLEASE NOTE: Strava data for our rides must be open (not private or hidden). Please use a heart rate monitor so that we can monitor correlated power and heart rate to help assure fair riding.

You can use our Discord channel to talk to each other at https://discord.gg/bPsQCNh

Please see the 6Points Zwift page link at https://6pointschallenges.com for more, including how we support our chosen charities.

https://6pointschallenges.com/

#6points

description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: DIRT 505 Thursday Endurance Ride
description:
Welcome to the DIRT 505 Thursday Endurance Ride!

Please join us weekly on Thursdays at a pace of 2.6-2.8w/kg for 90 minutes.

The 505 rides take place Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday for 90 minutes at the same time and same consistent pace. Saturday's we ride for 120 minutes.

We kick off our rides with a 5 minute pace at 2.2w/kg to get us warmed up and together as a team. The fence is used throughout the ride to keep everyone together. The fence will not kick you off the ride but we do encourage all our riders to stay together as close to the beacon as possible. Our rides offer a 30 minute late join option.
The ride leader will stay as close to ride pace as possible. We try to change up the routes each week and some of the routes may include some small to moderate climbs.

Our group exists to encourage and build up cyclists around the world no matter your time zone, fitness level, pro or amateur. We exist to encourage and build up a team culture of unity in the cycling community.

https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwiftdirt

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Team Zwift Philippines)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is hosted by Team Zwift Philippines (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/4f16b64b-60d5-414e-a4fc-91d6924ab305).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You’ll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that’s rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you’ll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL’s website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #ote

name: The Chop by AHDR
description:
AHDR THE CHOP

Being "in the chop" is an Aussie expression that you won't read about in the UCI rulebook. It's one of the many nuances of bike racing that only comes with experience.

Sometimes when you're in a race and find yourself in the final break-away bunch you might get asked "are we in the chop?" or "are we chopping it?". This is referring to collusion between the lead riders to work together until the finish. Many times a break-away will be doomed in the final kilometres because everyone starts playing cat and mouse, attacking, sitting up, etc.

But all that goes out the window in the last lap. This is generally where the group comes together and the final push begins. Working together goes out the window and is every man or woman for themselves for the final sprint to the line.

AHDR The Chop is a flat fast time-based handicap race. Do you always get blown out the back on the start of a multi grade race trying to hang onto the guy going with A grade pushing 5w/kg. Well not on The Chop. Released in Grades there are time splits from D to A with the intention of all coming together at the end for the big finish. Each Grade wears a different AHDR/Zwift kit making each grade easily identifiable on who's wheel you want to catch if they go past.

Races happen on Thursday evenings. We plan to add further races in the future. No single race has been the same. Many variables are at play on The Chop including course (which is generally a flat course), race numbers, and the inevitable sandbaggers. It has been called a team time trail and cat and mouse but the real racers know it only as "THE CHOP". Do you have what it takes to take the glory?

A Grade – AHDR GIANT Kit
B Grade – HDR Kit
C Grade – Fluoro Pink
D Grade – Fluoro Camo

#ahdr #cyclingaustralia

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: QT2 Systems Group Workout
description:
Join QT2 Systems for a challenging hour-long session, each Thursday morning at 5:15 AM EST, around some of the best routes on Zwift. Each 60-minute ride includes a quick warm-up and cool down. These workouts are designed to challenge and make you stronger along the way. One of our coaches is present, each week, to answer questions, motivate, and encourage riders throughout the entire workout.

With ZWIFT’s ride banding, these workouts are suitable for athletes of all levels, regardless of fitness level. That’s right, we are all “rubber-banded” together, relative to percentage of rider FTP.

QT2 Systems is one of the world’s premier triathlon coaching brands, and parent company to OutRival Racing, The Cycling Formula, The Core Diet, The Run Formula, and Cycle Life Studios.

description:

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

name: 5K Down Under
description:
Join us for a 5k group run led by Australian age-group triathlete Ben Shepherd. Ben is an avid triathlete and Zwifter who - like many of us - balances his training with work and family. He's a thirty-something father of a young daughter and credits triathlon with helping him lose over 50lb (25kg). He's a Ironman World Championships finisher (2016) and has qualified to represent his native Australia at the ITU Age-Group World Championships in September of this year.

After suffering a tendon injury to his patella (kneecap), he's working back to fitness with 100% treadmill running and is happy to have some company! Join him for a relaxed pace 5K. Come just to run or to chat about triathlon, Zwift, or where to get the best flat whites. Ben will be leading at 5:30/km (8:50/mi) pace, but feel free to join in at your own pace. This is a social run, not a race, so leave the racing flats at home for now.

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: Film My Run 500 with NoblePro
description:
Join Stephen Cousins and NoblePro for a 500 metre climb. Set your treadmill to 12% incline and run 4.2km of distance to reach 500 metres elevation. If your treadmill doesn't go that high, set it as high as it will go and join us anyway!

The run will be progressive starting at a walking pace of 5kph rising to 7kph for the final kilometre. Get on to the balls of your toes and get your calf muscles to do the work. This continuous uphill effort will help build strength and endurance for those hilly trail runs

This event is sponsored by the ever popular Noble Pro treadmills. You can get a discount on a NoblePro SMART treadmill from this link https://zwift.run/noble-pro

You can watch the run on the live stream at http://youtube.com/ZwiftRunChannel

Join the FMR Run Club for more fun events at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/6afca04c-8fac-4ffa-b700-f27a73d7f3dd/join

All levels are welcome and supported -- and don't worry! Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

name: GRIT Tempo-ish Thursday
description:
This is a group ride that has competitive sprint segments. Although not mandatory, riders are encouraged to participate in sprints. After each sprint we quickly regroup and continue the tempo paced lap. The motto of the ride has always been "Faster and farther together". Come have fun with us as we train to improve sprinting capability.

We ride a course that is approx. 5 km (3 miles) and we continue laps for 60 minutes. Each lap has a timed sprint zone and we sprint at this area on each lap for about 7 sprints in the 60 minute ride.

The tempo pace is around 3.0 w/kg and we normally have a large peloton of 30+ riders moving at this pace.

Sprints are short duration (10 - 30 seconds) and will be competitive as riders use maximum power to compete for the fastest sprint time.

name: Phoenix Rising As One
description:
Please join us on Phoenix Rising As One and, at least for a little while, overcome some of those annoying real life stresses. Phoenix Rising As One is a joyful social group ride. We welcome riders of all types and strengths to join us for an hour of fun and camaraderie. 

The yellow beacon will attempt to ride at a pace of between 1.5 and 1.8 w/kg.  There will be no fence and we welcome riders to ride at whatever pace they want – the objective of the ride is to be together which we are via text and discord.  And we will have a fab group of sweepers to accompany and inspire riders who wish to ride at a gentler pace.  We intend that no one ride alone unless they want to.

We will ride a variety of routes, attempting to cover some of the less traveled routes in Zwift and earning the occasional badge.  We will ride flattish routes although some nasty shorter climbs or non paved surfaces will inevitably be included. 

So wherever you are located, please join us for a rollicking fun hour of cycling.

For more information on all our weekly events, join our club Phoenix Rising on Zwift Companion.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/mNMva8ekJR
Check us out on Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/615972979730125/

description:
ZLDR Easy Breezy 8kph Run


Calling all Runners and Walkers - We want you! 


Round out your week with our Easy Breezy 8kph run. Whether you are shaking off of hard workout or just looking for a chill jog, our Thursday run is all about enjoying the journey at a relaxed pace. Join us as we chat, laugh, and embrace the pace. No rush, just vibes!


Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a relaxed 6.4 km run. Whether you want to use this time to walk just to get your blood flowing or push for a new personal best or anything in between, this is a great session to join! Even if you can't join for the full distance, come for whatever you have available. We are a flexible group of walkers/runners who want to help you get consistent and get results.


ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

name: CRYO-GEN: The Tempo Split
description:
Push your limits, find your rhythm.
Welcome to The Tempo Split, a dynamic tempo session designed to help you build aerobic power and endurance. This isn’t just a group ride; it’s a choose-your-own-adventure workout where you decide how deep to dig.

How it Works
We all start together in a single pen for a 10-20 minute progressive warm-up at a controlled, social pace. Once the warm-up is complete, the “Split” begins.
The Yellow Beacon (Lead): Will begin intervals at a high-tempo pace of 3.2 – 3.5 w/kg. This is for those looking to challenge their sustained power.
The Red Beacon (Sweep): Will begin intervals at a steady tempo pace of 2.7 – 3.0 w/kg. This is the safety net for those who want a solid workout without going “into the red.”

The Challenge
Start with the Yellow Beacon. Your goal is to hold that higher intensity for as long as possible. If the effort becomes unsustainable or your heart rate climbs too high, don’t worry—simply ease back to the Red Beacon following behind and finish the interval with that group.

Course & Intensity Notes
Please note that this is a flexible training session:
Terrain Impact: The ride format and group cohesion may change based on the route’s profile. Expect paces to shift slightly on climbs or descents.
Variable Intervals: Interval intensity and duration are subject to change week-to-week based on the specific goals of the session and the total distance of the chosen course.

Pro Tip: Use the draft! Whether you’re with the Yellow or Red group, staying tucked in a pack will help you maintain these tempo zones more efficiently.
Pro Tip: Keep an eye on the on-screen chat. The Beacons will announce interval starts, finishes, and any adjustments to the target w/kg based on the terrain.

Ride Rules
Be Kind: This is a supportive training environment.
Listen to your body: There is no shame in dropping back to the Red Beacon; that’s exactly why it’s there!
Stay Predictable: Avoid sudden surges so the groups can stay formed.

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: DWGZ Endurance - Mambo
description:
Welcome to DWGZ, a global community fueled by passion, grit, and the love of shared endurance! Our daily (Monday to Friday) endurance rides are  for those who thrive on long efforts, pushing limits, and embracing the burn together.
B Category rolls at a toppy paces of 3.2-4.0 w/kg average at the finish.
C Category rolls at a toppy pace of 2.8 – 3.2 w/kg average at the finish.
DWGZ are ideal for those looking to build stamina while holding a steady yet demanding effort.  Whether you ride with the beacon, or hammer with the fliers, you're sure to have a great time.  We take pride in cultivating rider engagement and a welcoming global community of fun fitness focused peeps.   
Expect consistent pacing, a spicy vibe and a supportive pack of dedicated riders committed to pushing the limits.

No matter where you are in the world, ride with us, suffer with us, and most importantly—finish strong with us.  DWGZ—where endurance, community and passion come together. Welcome to DWGZ ~  Get Gritty! 🚴🔥

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Know No Bounds Ride
description:
Welcome to the #KnowNoBounds Ride! A ride where we explore the boundaries (or lack thereof) on Zwift.
The ride will begin with 30 minutes at 3 w/kg, utilizing the fence to keep riders together. At the end of the 30 minutes, riders are free to do as they please:
they can stay together and explore the chosen course for the day, or they can head off to the nearest mountain with friends and race it!

When the 30 minute "fenced" portion of the ride is over, we will #KnowNoBounds. Have bike, will travel.
If needed, you will be able to turn to ride in opposite direction on the circuit to reconnect with the group quicker (if you need restroom or are dropped),
and most importantly, the group is not limited to the in-game courses. We can choose to go up a mountain, along a riverside, or any road we want to!

We’re on a mission: to set everyday cyclists free to explore without limits. And we’re going by bike.

Community and camaraderie in cycling is everything: group chat on this ride is highly encouraged!

name: EVOLVE 25km Road Race
description:
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 25, an electrifying 25km race on Zwift, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair
atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply
staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: Japan ZWIFT Hill Climb Race
description:
Welcome to the Japan Zwift Hill Climb Race!
This race is intended to provide an Hill Climb race on Thursdays
Let's enjoy!! All are welcome. 

POWER UPs:Not allowed
BIKES:All allowed

GROUPS:
Use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg then pick the correct group:
A:4.0 W/kg FTP or higer
B:3.2 W/kg to 4.0W/kg FTP
C:2.5 W/kg to 3.1W/kg FTP
D:Under2.4 W/kg FTP

Have Fun! Ride On!


Japan ZWIFT Hill Climb Race 

毎週木曜日に開催のヒルクライムレースになります。
Watopiaの「Epic KOM」「Volcano KOM」などのKOMルートをはじめとする多数のルートで
日本人ヒルクライマーが多数参戦し全てのカテゴリーの方が楽しめるレース展開に!?

世界中のZwifterと競い合うことでワークアウト以上の刺激とトレーニング効果が期待できます。

Zwifterの参加をお待ちしています。

POWER UPs:アイテム使用不可
BIKES:全ての自転車を選択可能

カテゴリーに関して
あなたのFTPを体重で割った数値をパワーウエイトレシオ【○○w/kg】となります。
例：FTP250W、体重60kgの方は4.166w/kgになりますのでAカテゴリーを選択下さい。

FTPとはサイクリストが1時間を通して維持できるパワーになります。
FTPの数値がわからない方はZwift Workout内のFTPテストで計測出来ます。

A:4.0 W/kg FTP 以上の方
B:3.2 W/kg～4.0W/kg FTPの方
C:2.5 W/kg～3.1W/kg FTPの方
D:2.4 W/kg FTP以下の方

Have Fun! Ride On!!

Facebook:https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftridersjp/

#ZwiftJapan #ZwiftWarriorsCycling #FunisFast #japanrace
#japan #zwc

name: DIRT Doozy Race
description:
Join team DIRT as we race around the finest flat or hilly courses Zwift has
to offer. Not too long, but not too short. This race is meant to bring
your best. All categories start together, so be ready for a fast start and
hard racing.

Final results will be available on zwiftpower.com. Racers riding outside
of their assigned Zwiftpower category will be disqualified and not count
towards official results

Team DIRT is an acronym for Dads Inside Riding Trainers. We are a
brotherhood like-minded moms and dads who love to make time for zwift
around work and family. We are a social racing team consisting mostly of
mid-level zwifters. The Watts Up Wednesday Ride idea came about because a
lot of us have work and family obligations during the week and this would
be a perfect way to squeeze in a great ride.

Don't forget to add "DIRT" after your name and make sure you are not on a
TT bike.

Please join us on Discord at https://discord.gg/sUnwqPZ

And, Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZWIFTDIRT

name: GXY BANDWAGON – (CAT D)
description:
ALL INCLUSIVE BANDED SOCIAL GROUP RIDE.
TIME TO RELEASE THE PRESSURES OF MODERN DAY LIFE WITH THIS DAILY FUN GET
TOGETHER.
THESE EVENTS WILL BE LAP OR TIME BASED GENERALLY UP TO 90 MINUTES IN DURATION.
(CELEBRITY SWEEPER INCLUDED)
GXY are a friendly, open and inclusive cycling club, who ride together virtually and in real life. Whether you ride
socially, race, train with intent or just like to mix it up, you are always welcome.
Here, together.
https://discord.com/channels/922817854372478986/1472960938612101150
If you would like to join our community, you can find us here:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 11
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: So Happy It's Thursday - DIRT Ride (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
Welcome to the Thursday morning So Happy It's Thursday DIRT Ride.

Please join us weekly on Thursday mornings for a C level 90 minute group ride.

C category: 2.3 - 2.8 w/kg

Team DIRT is an acronym for Dads Inside Riding Trainers. This group has grown and brought like minded moms and dads together who love to make time for Zwift around work and family.

Please join us on discord: https://discord.gg/sUnwqPZ

And Facebook at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZWIFTDIRT

name: Team RWB - The Irish are everywhere! ! Base Ride
description:
Hi Folks. What`s the Craic ?

Welcome to Riders Without Borders (RWB) base ride.

This is an open category ride without a beacon or sweep. So your cooperation is needed in building the various groupettos. Average power output can be anywhere between 1.5 and 4.0 W/kg.
- Late Start, will also be on offer.

There will be no official sweep, but we will be happy to see riders helping one another out.

The Craic, Being Irish we put a high value on entertaining chat. So let`s be hearing from you. Racing Without Borders, has its origins in Ireland and is as the name
suggests; open to all. So, if you have ever supped a pint of Guinness, we consider you one of us. We have around 20 teams, across all categories racing in the ZRL European zone (EMEA).

We also have ambitions to put a few teams out in the USA (Americas East). So, if you are interested in testing yourself, get in touch with us on the links below.

Discord link: 
https://discord.gg/aezej3cR 

Website: 
www.withoutborders.online 

Come join Team RWB on Zwift: 
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/TeamRWB/home 

Find Us on Facebook: 
www.facebook.com/rwbcycling 

Join our Zwiftpower Team: 
www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=3369 

Join our Strava Group: 
www.strava.com/clubs/480584 

Join our Cycling Ireland UCI Club: 
https://withoutborders.online/index.php/cycling-ireland-affiliation/ 

#racingwithoutborders

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: BMTR Banded Warmup
description:
Let's warm up and enjoy riding together as one group before today's BMTR Chase Adventure event, which has four categories/groups: A, B, C, and D. The event starts in 35 minutes.

Since this is a "banded" event, everyone can warm up at their own pace while the Zwift "Rubberband" feature keeps us together.

Although there won't be any official Yellow or Red Beacons for this event, you'll see familiar BMTR Ride Leaders and Sweeps joining you for some warm-up fun!

JOIN US
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bmtrzwiftclub
- Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/BMTRZwiftClub
- Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/BMTR/home

ALL WEEKLY BMTR EVENTS
- https://bmtr.events

name: EMU Japan Short Race
description:
(Japanese follows)
Welcome to the EMU Japan Short Race!

・POWER UPs:Allowed at all times
・BIKES: No TT bike
・CATEGORY: Use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg then pick the correct group

A:4.0 W/kg FTP+
B:3.2 W/kg to 4.0 W/kg FTP
C:2.5 W/kg to 3.1 W/kg FTP
D:Under 2.4 W/kg FTP

All racers using zPower will be DQ
Heart rate monitors compulsory for all categories

The following persons may be DQ by the organizer's authority.
・Obviously wrong category
・Riding in an unrealistic manner

Final results will be based on ZwiftPower.
Have Fun! Ride On!!

EMU Japan Short Raceにようこそ！ EMUで人気のあるコースを中心に選択し、コースは毎週変わります！
みなさんの参加をお待ちしています。

・POWER UPs: アイテム使用可能
・BIKES: TTバイクは使用不可

・カテゴリーに関して
あなたのFTPを体重で割った数値をパワーウエイトレシオ【○○W/kg】となります。
例：FTP250W、体重60kgの方は4.166W/kgになりますのでAカテゴリーを選択下さい。
FTPとはサイクリストが1時間を通して維持できるパワーになります。 FTPの数値がわからない方はZwift Workout内のFTPテストで計測出来ます。

A:4.0W/kg FTP以上の方
B:3.2W/kg～4.0W/kg FTPの方
C:2.5W/kg～3.1W/kg FTPの方
D:2.4W/kg FTP以下の方

・リザルト
公正なレースを行うために、最終的なリザルトはZwiftpower.comといたします。

・注意事項
レース中の誹謗中傷コメントや、妨害やその他、スポーツマンシップに反する行為を行った参加者を結果から除外することができます。

・イベントの詳細は、注意事項はEMUのTwitterでも随時お知らせしていますので、ご確認お願いいたします。
https://twitter.com/emu_speed

#EMUSpeedClub #ZwiftJapan

name: Eastern Canadian Quest Racing Series (ECQRS)
description:
Welcome to the Eastern Canadian Quest Racing Series (ECQRS).  Expect each race to be highly competitive.  For a fair race, all racers using zPower, lacking heart rate monitors, or riding unrealistically can be disqualified.  We at ECQ hope you enjoy our racing series.  Riders who have not joined ZwiftPower and agreed to the data storage and sharing conditions will be excluded from the final results.
Please add ECQRS to your name.
#ECQRS 
#seeyouontheroads.

name: Fearless Flying Feet Track Intervals
description:
Ready to get your feet flying by running track intervals of 800 meters followed by a 400 meter recovery. FIVE total times to gain speed. Challenge yourself to get better with each interval and come back week after week!

"He who would learn to fly one day must first learn to stand and walk and run and climb and dance; one cannot fly into flying.” Friedrich Nietzsche
https://www.brainyquote.com/quotes/friedrich_nietzsche_159166

#fear

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Group Run 5k
description:
DESCRIPTION

Join us for a 5k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW (D)

name: SROZ's Contenders, Pretenders, & Rear-Enders
description:
Whether you’re a Tour de France hopeful, a procrastinating amateur, or the person whose front tire spends a suspicious amount of time in someone else’s rear derailleur...we honestly don't care!

There are no "real" awards for winning a climb or clinching a sprint. If you’re looking for a trophy, try a middle school soccer tournament.

The goal is to maintain a steady engine...smooth rotations and clean pulls. Even when the profile gets punchy or the wind shifts, we're staying "dialed in" on 18–20 mph (30–32 kph).

Feeling a little gapped? Don't sweat it. We strive to have a dedicated sweeper on every ride to help pull you back into the fold.

The "Gene Autry" Philosophy: We believe in freedom! As the legend himself said, we won’t fence you in.

At the end of the day, you’re the captain of your own bike. Whether you’re pushing the pace or soaking in the scenery: ride your ride!

So come out, enjoy the virtual fresh air, and keep the faith!

eventSubgroups.name: SROZ's Contenders, Pretenders, & Rear-Enders (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
We don’t care if you’re a contender, pretender, or rear-ender!  We just enjoy riding with others in a fun, social group. 

There are no “real” awards for being first on a climb or on a sprint during our rides, only the satisfaction of being able to ride a bike for just one more day! 

The pace may vary depending on the course, but we will strive to ride and socialize with an overall average pace of approximately 18-20mph (30-32kph).  

We will try to have a sweeper on each ride to help any rider who may want to return to the group. 

Also, we will be applying the Gene Autry philosophy…we won’t fence you in!

Remember, the choice is yours…ride your ride!

So, enjoy your ride, keep the faith, and become that someone!

name: Hercules - The Thursday Social
description:
Join our Thursday Social 5k - a super friendly and inclusive group run on Zwift!

You can walk, jog or race, everyone is welcome.

Expect great chat, good vibes, and a welcoming atmosphere for all runners, whether you're new to Zwift or a seasoned pro.

Please join our Zwift Club at https://www.zwift.com/uk/clubs/d6581e47-deb2-46ed-a278-5777d5d2fd0f/home

Please join our Facebook Group at https://www.facebook.com/groups/2606407819659263/

Please join us online at https://herculesrunningandtri.com/

Please join us on Strava at https://www.strava.com/clubs/herculesrunningandtriclub

If you would like to own our famous Pink & Black Club Kit in real life, please order at https://tinyurl.com/herculestrirun

name: BMTR Chase Adventure
description:
Why is this event called a “Chase Adventure?” Because it allows each participant to explore the upper reaches of their personal power map (zones) — primarily Zones 3 and 4 — while collaborating with other like-minded explorers on a similar journey, all sharing the same goal: to get stronger, fitter, and faster. This “adventure” gives each participant an opportunity to expand their personal “power map” and reach new heights of achievement. 

As BMTR is known for having the “highest chat per kilogram” on Zwift 😀, participants will also enjoy fun, friendly banter and benefit from the support and camaraderie of their teammates and ride leader as they navigate this “adventure” together!

Ride Leaders will coordinate the B, C, and D Category teams to keep their effort levels within the advertised average w/kg range. The A Category team will self-organize their effort level.


**HOW THE "CHASE" IS SET UP**
Four teams will start at different intervals: Team D goes first, followed by Teams C,  B, and finally A. The goal is for Teams A, B, and C to catch up with Team D near the finish line by working as a Team at their designated w/kg paces

When faster teams catch up to slower ones, riders can join them to increase their effort. Faster team members can also drop back to recover. If a rider can't keep up, they can return to their original group and try again the following week! 


**SWITCHING TEAMS AND SWEEPING DURING THE LATE JOIN PERIOD**
You have a 30-minute Late Join period to switch teams if your current team isn't at your desired pace. During this time, we won't conduct any sweeps, so please use the Late Join feature to rejoin your category group if you fall behind. To rejoin or switch teams, do not “quit” the event; instead, simply “exit/end,” save your ride, then rejoin your original team category or select a different one.


**AVERAGE W/KG RANGE CATEGORY CALCULATIONS**
The specified average watts per kilogram (w/kg) pace ranges for each Team represent the overall average for the entire event. During the event, teams may, at times, exceed or fall below this range. The respective category event averages are calculated to keep most riders within Zone 3 and 4 effort levels. This assumes that each rider selects a category that accurately reflects both their Functional Threshold Power (FTP) and their Zwift-assigned category.

We look forward to having you join us!


**Join Us On Any or All of the Following Platforms!**
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/bmtrzwiftclub
- Strava: https://www.strava.com/clubs/BMTRZwiftClub
- Zwift: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/BMTR/home
- Instagram: https://instagram.com/bmtrclub
 
**To Find All Weekly BMTR Events** 
- https://bmtr.events

name: The Feed Community Ride
description:
Join us on for a morning Feed community ride!

There will be a chance to win Feed store credit during the ride, and all participants receive an exclusive The Feed kit in their Zwift garage for completing the event.

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Thursday Pizza Burner  🍕🔥
description:
Burn those pizza slices, chat about all things Zwift/cycling, and build your base in this ride led by Zwift Insider's Eric Schlange! Each week we spin through 100km of pristine Zwift roads, rotating between flattish routes to keep the endurance training fast and fun.

PACING:
Eric will ride at ~2.5w/kg/210W for an average speed of 37-39kph (23-24mph). Join his yellow beacon group to ride at the stated pace, join the "zinners" up the road for something spicier, or do whatever keeps you motivated. It's all good!

VOICE CHAT:
Longer rides always pass faster with voice chat, so join us on Discord if you're able! Find us at https://discord.gg/zwiftinsider

KIT UNLOCK:
You will unlock the Zwift Insider kit upon finishing this event.

name: Outseason Thursdays with Endurance Nation
description:
Transform Your Winter with Endurance Nation’s OutSeason Program!

Get ready to build next-level fitness with our 9-week OutSeason cycling series! Join us every Tuesday and Thursday for a focused, one-hour session designed to boost your strength, endurance, and power. With just two workouts per week, you’ll start building winter strength that will prepare you to reach your goals next season. This program is tried-and-true, with over 10,000 athletes using it to succeed in cycling and triathlon for over 16 years.

Why settle for base miles alone when you can add targeted intensity this winter? Join Endurance Nation and see what a season of smart training can do. Discover more about the OutSeason at www.endurancenation.us/zwift.

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides on Saturdays, Sundays and Mondays each week — they are great alternates to these scheduled workouts!

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: TOWER 26 Group Workout
description:
TOWER 26 Workout: Adding to Your Tool Box

This weeks Tower 26 workout focuses on tempo designed to get you riding FAST and STRONG!

Join EVERY Tuesday and Thursday on Zwift while being COACHED on Zoom. Subscribe to the monthly workouts here.

https://training.tower26.com/t-26-cycling-training-program

TOWER 26 Swimming, the premiere name in triathlon swim training, is now offering triathlon coaching services. Led by experienced Head Coach Jim Lubinski, these triathlon specific cycling workouts will follow the TOWER 26 season-long progression of phases of training building your cycling strength in a smart, periodized manner. Our phases of training entail: Foundation, Sharpening, Race Preparation, Race Ready, and Recovery/Technical. Each workout will fit within the current phase of training, educating the athlete as to how the specific workout fits into the overall season-long progression and how the workout will best get the athlete Performance Prepared and Race READY.

Listen to the TOWER 26 - Be Race Ready Podcast with Coach Jim Lubinski and Coach Gerry Rodrigues help further your triathlon education in an entertaining spin on it.

For more information on TOWER 26 go to www.TOWER26.com or email

eventSubgroups.description:
TOWER 26 Workout: Adding to Your Tool Box

This weeks Tower 26 workout focuses on tempo designed to get you riding FAST and STRONG!

Join EVERY Tuesday and Thursday on Zwift while being COACHED on Zoom. Subscribe to the monthly workouts here.

https://training.tower26.com/t-26-cycling-training-program

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 12
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: ZLDR AYOP Hour Run
description:
Join Zwift Long-Distance Runners (ZLDR) run club for a 1 hour AYOP (at your own pace) run. Whether you want to push for a new personal best, use it as part of your weekly training program or just enjoy a sociable run this is suitable for you.

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn

#zldr_run

name: Virginia's Blue Ridge TWENTY28 Road to LA
description:
Virginia’s Blue Ridge TWENTY28 are now in our 20th year of operation developing high-performance athletes and the next generation of competitive cyclists. Success in aligning student-athletes with college scholarships through cycling. Support of the multi-discipline athlete. Adaptive, dynamic, versatile, resilient, and committed to growing diversity in the landscape of women’s cycling.

When the team was formed in 2005, The goal of TWENTY12 was to create a pathway for athletes to the 2012 London Olympics. London resulted in two Olympic medals; gold and silver, the only U.S based road program to achieve this. The team replicated its success with 5 medals in Rio 2016 Olympics/Paralympics, two in Tokyo, two in Paris 2024, and now on to LA 2028.

Ride Leaders + Sweepers include
Amanda Coker who is a level 100 Zwifter and is an accomplished ultra-endurance IRL athlete: World Record Holder - 86,573.2 miles ridden in a year - yes that’s riding 237 miles per day for 365 straight days

Sweepers include members of Virginia’s Blue Ridge TWENTY24 Pro Cycling and regulars are team founder Nicola Cranmer and TWENTY24 eSports captain Kristin Kulchinsky

Find us on social media
Web: http://teamtwenty24.com/
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/twenty28procycling/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/twenty24procycling

To join our club, please follow the link provided: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/5b278b37-bb5c-41fd-9834-cde077342a0c/home

eventSubgroups.name: Virginia's Blue Ridge TWENTY28 Road to LA (D)

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Ascenders Social Thursday Rides
description:
Ascenders Social Thursday Rides are presented in two styles to meet your choice of pace. You can either join the faster "C" Group or chill out with the "D" Group while we look forward to having you with us for 90 minutes of rolling good time.

Join us on FaceBook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
Discord Channel: https://discord.com/invite/cBfQ5jkrPh

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Social Thursday Rides (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
Ascenders Team Social Thursday comes in two flavors to meet your choice of pace. Join either the faster "C" Group or chill out with the "D" Group and we look forward to having you with us for 90minutes of rolling good time.

Join the Ascenders Team FB group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift

eventSubgroups.description: Ascenders Team Social Thursday comes in two flavors to meet your choice of pace. Join either the faster "C" Group or chill out with the "D" Group and we look forward to having you with us for 90minutes of rolling good time.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Endure ES Base Building
description:
The goal of this ride is to focus on building your aerobic base, by riding in your zone 2 power output. There is no ride leader, so we encourage you to group up with other riders of similar ability along the way. Remember, this is not a race!

If you enjoy this ride and want easy access to future events we host, make sure to join our club by clicking the following link: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/3510fe3d-531b-4a4a-8505-64d200733aec/home

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: EZR Thursday Fun Ride
description:
All riders are welcome to join the EZ Riders (EZR) for this relaxed pace social group ride. Whether you are new to Zwift or group rides, looking for a recovery/rehab ride, or just looking to ride with a supportive group of friends, this ride is for you. The ride leader will be staying within the advertised average pace of this ride as we explore Zwift’s various worlds. Please respect the ride leader’s pace and ride with the yellow beacon.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/kmfS4YN

Facebook Page: http://www.facebook.com/groups/zer.cycling

#zer #ezr #ezriders
