name: TSE POKER CHALLENGE 2/4
description:
TSE Poker Challenge: Virtual Cycling Competition
The TSE Poker Challenge is a virtual cycling competition organized by the TSE club, taking
place every Thursday in March. The event uses a "tiny" format, consisting of short mini-stages.

Calendar and Structure
The competition includes 4 events, each made up of 4 mini-stages lasting approximately 15
minutes each:
● March 12: Set in France (Paris), featuring stages such as the Champs-Élysées and Ven Top.
● March 19: Set in Scotland, with routes like the Glasgow Crit and Scotland Smash.
● March 26: Set in Watopia (e.g., Volcano Circuit, Mayan San Remo).
● April 2: Set between Makuri Islands and Watopia.

Race Times (Italian Time): 19:05, 19:25, 19:45, and 20:05.

Regulations and Technical Requirements
The event is open to categories A, B, C, and D with Category Enforcement active.
● Equipment: You may choose your preferred bike, but Time Trial (TT) bikes are
prohibited.
● Mandatory: A heart rate monitor is required; zPower is not allowed.
● Settings: Drafting and steering are enabled, while Powerups are disabled.
● Scoring: A dynamic system is used based on the number of participants (e.g., 60 starters
= 60 points for 1st place, 1 point for the last).
● Penalties: Athletes who change categories during the tour will receive penalties.

Prizes and Benefits
● General Classification: Based on combined results from the four weeks.
● Real Prizes: Epicasport provides prizes for the overall winners.
● Discount: All participants unlock a 30% discount (on a minimum spend of €75) at
www.epicasport.com.

For technical support or team registrations, contact the organizers at 

Official Sites: * www.tsezwift.com * www.epicasport.com

name: ZZRC GROOVY TRAIN - Rock n' Roll
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Groovy Train 2.5-2.7wkg. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2.5-2.7 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride is as advertised

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .

Sweepers are not in operation on this ride so please consider using late join in the first 30 mins or make a small group of your own.

We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord

TOP TIPS

name: Hotchillee Gain Train
description:
What is it?
Firstly, IT’S NOT A RACE but that doesn’t mean it’s an easy pace no-drop ride. It’s a paced “Category +” Hare and Hounds where you will be working predominately at the upper end of your category and hanging on for as long as you can in search of those gains.

What are the paces?
Paces are based on what the Ride Leader for each group will average and our leaders are pretty accurate - check on ZwiftPower afterwards.   Buffet Class (D+) Leader will average 2.4 w/kg (departs at 1830)
Second Class (C+) Leader average 3.1 w/kg (departs at 1834)
First Class (B+) Leader will average 3.7 w/kg (departs at 1836)

Please do not push the pace faster than this, it ruins it for everyone - respect your Ride Leader, do what they ask and don’t sandbag.

Will the groups catch each other?
Usually we aim for there to be a catch/merge in the final 5km or so but this does depend on the course.
If a merge does happen, stronger riders are encouraged to “upgrade” to the faster group and, if you’re struggling, you can also “downgrade” to a slower one.

What if I get dropped?
Suck it up, maybe find someone to spin home with and try to hang on a bit longer next week - just like a IRL chain-gang, that’s how you get stronger.

Can I push it at the end?
With about 3km to go, your Leader will give a Go! Go! Go! shout and then, if you want, you can empty the tank.

  What is Hotchillee?
Hotchillee is both an events company that organises some of the world's best cycling events and a cycling community. Check out the website for details of events, rides and to get involved www.hotchillee.com

name: ZHCC RUBBERBAND CLUB
description:
Want a 100% guaranteed no drop ride? want a 100% guarantee you can ride at your own pace? want a 100% guarantee you WILL stay in the group,? want a 100% guarantee that the nasty fence will NOT kick you...........THEN LOOK NO FURTHER, this is the ride for you.
Try it - YOU MIGHT JUST LIKE IT!
Our ZHCC Rubber Band Club has the “rubber-band” activated which means that no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. The rubber band allows you to ride at your own pace, slow or fast and you are guaranteed to stay in the group.
The ride speed will be slightly less than you would normally experience in a group ride due to the rubber band effect, but we feel that's a small price to pay to allow all level of riders to remain together throughout the ride.
In this supportive environment it’s a great way of completing a ride knowing you wont get dropped or get dragged along by those naughty fliers!
ZHCC do not race each other, we ride as one, have fun and enjoy a group ride.
The ZHCC gang look forward to riding with you.
STRAVA : https://www.strava.com/clubs/518792
ZHCC Facebook page - https://www.facebook.com/groups/1163022533747527/Opens a new window
DISCORD : https://www.discord.gg/ukSdEQd

name: Glorious Cycling Club
description: Welcome to our second Glorious Cycling Club chase race. This is a fun a social handicap race where riders in each category (A, B, C, D & E) will work together to chase/stay away from the other categories. With the help of a coach on Discord we'll get you working seamlessly together. There can only be one race winner, however, so eventually it will be every rider for themselves across the finish line! This race is not part of the Glorious Cycling Club racing league and so there are no league points available. It's just for fun!

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 22
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: ZLDR Progression Event
description:
Welcome to our ZLDR Progression Event. Today we’re going to do a progression run, which basically starts a run at a slower pace and finishes at a faster pace than you started. So we’ll get progressively faster with each split. Progression runs help us build our endurance base but with less stress on our bodies than a typical interval or tempo run. This can help reduce the risk of injury. Plus it helps break up the monotony in our training.

Progression running also will help teach you smarter pacing, starting conservatively so you don’t burn out too soon. It also will help train your body to get used to pushing harder at the end of a race.

This event will start with a 10 minute warm up followed by 4 x 5 mins splits. The first split will be at 10k pace and each split will be a stair step up at +2%. Finally we will cool down for 10 minutes. So let’s get those shoes laced up and ready to go!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your on-foot dreams and help you develop your running/walking skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook group:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Instagram:
https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Strava:
https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: EVO CC Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO CC race series.  Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Stage 4 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Sand and Sequoias
Date: April 6-12

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Sand and Sequoias, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: Zwift NL Group Ride
description:
Relaxed samen op pad, dat is waar het in onze ZwiftNL Group Rides om gaat. Rustig tempo en een makkelijke route, zodat jij ook een beetje kunt socializen met de andere deelnemers. Samen uit, samen thuis!
____________

Relaxed on the road together, that's what our ZwiftNL Group Rides are all about. Easy pace and an easy route, so you can also socialize a bit with the other participants. Out together, home together!

name: Hitting the Heights
description:
Join our weekly ride up one of Zwift's epic climbs, led by the Good Time Riders, team GTR.

Whether you’re a bona fide mountain goat, a climbing combatant aiming to improve, or merely persuading your legs that hills are good for you ……our hitting the heights ride is just what you need.

With climbing enthusiast and Alpine goat, Danny, at the helm alongside a great GTR team, you will get all the encouragement and support you need to hit the Zwift summits - and maybe even beat your personal goals - in style. Pack your va va voom and let’s ride!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: PACK🐺 Social + KOM After Party
description:
Join the PACK and experience a fun social group ride with an average lap pace of around 2w/kg. Everyone is welcome so come and enjoy the friendly supportive atmosphere with late join enabled if you miss the start. The Leader will always be at the correct pace, giving out instructions to help new riders as communication is the key to a good group ride.

After the event finishes, there is an optional fun After Party KOM challenge up the final climb. Ride with others or try to beat your HoloReplay.

All riders will appear in the same PACK kit (unlocks at end) and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:

name: TSE POKER CHALLENGE 3/4
description:
TSE Poker Challenge: Virtual Cycling Competition
The TSE Poker Challenge is a virtual cycling competition organized by the TSE club, taking
place every Thursday in March. The event uses a "tiny" format, consisting of short mini-stages.

Calendar and Structure
The competition includes 4 events, each made up of 4 mini-stages lasting approximately 15
minutes each:
● March 12: Set in France (Paris), featuring stages such as the Champs-Élysées and Ven Top.
● March 19: Set in Scotland, with routes like the Glasgow Crit and Scotland Smash.
● March 26: Set in Watopia (e.g., Volcano Circuit, Mayan San Remo).
● April 2: Set between Makuri Islands and Watopia.

Race Times (Italian Time): 19:05, 19:25, 19:45, and 20:05.

Regulations and Technical Requirements
The event is open to categories A, B, C, and D with Category Enforcement active.
● Equipment: You may choose your preferred bike, but Time Trial (TT) bikes are
prohibited.
● Mandatory: A heart rate monitor is required; zPower is not allowed.
● Settings: Drafting and steering are enabled, while Powerups are disabled.
● Scoring: A dynamic system is used based on the number of participants (e.g., 60 starters
= 60 points for 1st place, 1 point for the last).
● Penalties: Athletes who change categories during the tour will receive penalties.

Prizes and Benefits
● General Classification: Based on combined results from the four weeks.
● Real Prizes: Epicasport provides prizes for the overall winners.
● Discount: All participants unlock a 30% discount (on a minimum spend of €75) at
www.epicasport.com.

For technical support or team registrations, contact the organizers at 

Official Sites: * www.tsezwift.com * www.epicasport.com

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 23
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: FAAST CREW CHAIN GANG!
description:
COME JOIN THE CREW! https://taplink.cc/faastcrew
.
FAAST CREW GROUP RIDES! 🤘
Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday Zwift rides! Everyone is welcome to join… let’s have some fun and get them GAINZ!
.
TUESDAY FTP BUILDER! @ 18:45(gmt)
It’s time to put the work in… each week we will be aiming to push your limits with a workout aimed to boost those FTP numbers…
Come get involved and watch yourself get stronger each week.
.
WEDNESDAY SOCIAL RIDE! @ 18:45(gmt)
Social pace night (banded together)
The main aim of these rides will be to keep things easy and sociable… not only to allow for a wider range of capabilities to get involved… but if you’re looking to get stuck in on Thursdays ride, have yourself an active rest day and use this ride to get to know your riding buddies.
There will also be some “optional” watt bombs being dropped… get involved if you fancy some sprint training!
.
THURSDAY CHAIN GANG! @ 18:45(gmt)
Chain gang night (around 4w/kg)
Come get stuck in and let’s empty those tanks!
This will be a full gas effort… we work as a unit for the most part, let’s try keep things together and help keep the pack strong… and we will operate a sweeper up until the half way mark.
There will however be a sprint finish with results, so the last 5-10minutes it’s every man/woman for themselves. Get stuck in and try take the glory.
This will be a race format but the finish line is an imaginary one as the clock hits zero.
.
LATE JOIN is enabled, so you can join us up to 30 mins after the start time, with the event link.
.
Please help spread the word, invite your pals and be sure to tag us in anything you post… we like to see you suffering, so post on your story of you smashing those pedals and tag @faastcrew.
.
PLEASE JOIN OUR ZWIFT CLUB.
That’s where all the rides will be posted but if you happen to miss them make sure to follow our Instagram page and keep up to date with any upcoming events and announcements.
(All relevant links, click the tap link in bio)
.
We look forward to seeing you all there, come get involved and LET’S HAVE IT!
.
The FAAST CREW.

name: Team CLS High 'n' Low
description:
Workout for the session - https://www.teamclscc.co.uk/workouts/

Come and Join Team CLS for our Thursday Group Workout... A leader guided group workout which provides structure, flexibility and good banter, with regulars around you. 

Our Tuesday and Thursday workouts compliment each other and follow a progressive structure. If you stick with it you will see steady gains in fitness over the months.  

Go to https://www.teamclscc.co.uk/workouts where you can view / download information on the workout with all leader calls. We always allow the leaders to add their twist to things though as they try to work with the group so keep focussed.

name: DBR Thursday Race
description:
Welcome to DBR Thursday Race.
All will start with 1 minute between RS categories. Expect each race to be highly competitive and have fun.
Categories
Each race will be using Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) for categorization.
race up a category, if your RS is at the top end of a category. Or if you simply desire an added challenge.
- A: 1000 =< 690
- B:690<520
- C:520<350
- D:350<180
- E:180<=0
Your Zwift Racing Score is determined by how much success you have in races.
better against people with higher ZRS, then your ZRS may increase. If you place worse than people with lower ZRS, then your score might decrease. Read more about ZRS here - https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh <https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh>
If your ZRS increases past a category boundary during the series, your points earned will not transfer to the new category with you.
RULES:
Smart trainer or power meter is required. zPower will be removed.
Heart rate monitors are compulsory for all categories.
No TT bikes unless it's a TT event.
You need to have a registered account with Zwiftpower.com and accept their terms and conditions to be in the results of the race.
Misc.
Race organizers may request riders in all categories to provide a dual recording or a matching RL effort (Strava) in order to verify a Zwift race effort. Failure to comply may result in disqualification from the event. Failure to provide a dual recording or RL effort on three requests may result in disqualification from future events, until a dual recording with a matching effort is provided.
Race organizers may exclude a rider from the results, due to poor sportsmanship. Examples may be bad language, sandbagging or other behavior of a disruptive manner.

#danishbikeriders

name: GXY BLAST OFF! [1.2-1.6WKG] CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY BLAST OFF!
Please join today's launch, a gentle 60minutes at a beginners / recovery pace.
Blast off by name… but steady as we go, whilst we ride around predominantly flat or slightly undulating courses with
power and speed maintained to suite everyone on this GALAXY no drop ride.
A designated social discord channel is available to just chat or to learn from experienced leaders about how to get the
most out of your journey on the Zwift platform.
We ride as one under the guidance of an experienced leader and sweepers who are there to help and support you.
Their primary aim is of course to keep the group together whilst sharing knowledge and making the ride fun and
enjoyable for everyone.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed. We are looking forward to riding with you
on this, our launchpad ride.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link or by the email below:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: NO LIMIT CC - SUPER SOCIAL
description:
👥 No Limit Cycling Club is a community-led cycling club built around consistency, confidence, and riding together. Riders of all backgrounds and abilities are welcome.

💬 Super Social Thursdays are steady group rides designed to reset after the day. The ride is social and relaxed, with space to chat, sit in the draft, or simply enjoy riding together.

🗺 Routes rotate across Zwift worlds including Watopia, France, and Makuri Islands to keep rides fresh and fun.

🧭 Ride leads manage pacing and communication. Sweepers support riders at the back and help with regrouping when needed.

🔒We warm up around 1.5 w/kg, then settle into 1.8–2.2 w/kg. Fence is on to keep the group together and may be lifted towards the end.

🎽 Ride with NLCC to unlock and wear the official No Limit CC Zwift kit.

🔁 Other NLCC rides

Tue 7:00 PM GMT
Sat 7:00 AM GMT
Sun 9:05 AM GMT

📲 Contact

NLCC on Zwift:  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/469e53e5-462c-4aa7-b34c-026384533c0b/home

Strava: https://strava.app.link/Vq02OaxrDZb

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/nolimit.bike

name: CSPT club run
description:
Welcome to CSPT; a cycling and running club with a difference. It's a place where members can meet up & have fun without any pressure to perform.

Whether you're short of motivation or inspiration then CSPT is the place for you. Drawing on years of experience from all walks of life and athletic experience we can help to motivate you to achieve whatever your goal is.

With CSPT - "YOU CAN"

This event has no leader, no fence and no rules because that's not what we're about. Stay with the bunch, often we are banded, we'll help you just enjoy being part of the club.

Please join our club : CSPT for further details.

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Poursuite de la Lanterne Rouge p/b Velos
description:
Looking for a fun and well supported social ride that will make you stronger? Then join us on Poursuite de la Lanterne Rouge! This event is great for confident D riders looking to step up to something a bit faster, as well as experienced riders who want to test themselves at race pace. We ride together for 45 minutes at 23mph/38kph (2.3w/kg), have a KOM to conquer then a Sprint to warm the legs, then we unleash our Red Beacon to step on the gas and - after a little head start to give our Lanterne Rouge a fighting chance - anyone game for a chase is free to fly. Three chase groups are offered with 3-6 minute deficits to close, and you are guaranteed to get stronger with each attempt to catch! The Yellow Beacon leader will continue at the listed pace the full hour to help those who don’t fancy a chase finish in friendly company. Strong sweep support is available for anyone needing a wheel get back into the leader’s group.
Join our social group at: www.teamvelos.co.uk

eventSubgroups.name: Poursuite de la Lanterne Rouge p/b Velos (C)

name: Renegade Race
description:
Renegade Race is intended to bring out your inner renegade.. Be fearless
and go for the brake away.

Challenge your opponents with fearless cycling.

We encourage you to race Clean with your true weight, so we all have a hard
and fair race every time we mount the bikes.

We dare you to race with the fastest participants.
So be ready from the start, you will experience hard starts, fearless
climbs and fast middle sections.

Results can be seen at zwiftpower.com and will be based on your category.

In order to make the racing as fair as possible, racers on zPower, lacking
heart rate monitors, riding an an inappropriate category,
or riding very unrealistically will be disqualified. Final results will be
available on zwiftpower.com.

Find us on FB : https://www.facebook.com/groups/873123543515963

name: Hudik Triathlon Time Trial
description:
Hudik Triathlon continues their summer TT-events inside during the colder winter months! 

Join us every Thursday for some high power TT’ing through Tempus Fugit, make the sand blaze and beat your own time or your opponents. 

See you in the pen!

name: Bens Bash
description:
Training ride open to all. The aim of these rides is to provide a regular training ride for all to join in, riding at a steady pace that for some will feel challenging but over time you will get fitter and more comfortable.

Be mindful of your pace and try to keep the group together rather than just ride off on your own - look at this as a way to improve your fitness as well as pick up some skills around riding with others. 

Try not to ride over 3.5 w/kg but if you are feeling frisky - feel free to open the taps with 10 mins to go. 

We will vary the routes so check back regularly.

name: Mountain Massif's Mountain Goat Chase Race
description:
This is a race with a difference. The slowest riders will start first with a time advantage over the fastest riders and race up to the top of the climb.
To make it fair, each Category’s advantage will decrease slightly, over the next.
The objective is to ride hard and try and reach the top of the climb, before being caught. This is a unique event because do you form a group and work together on the climb, or do you strike out alone? These decisions will impact the outcome.

Rules:
Heart Rate monitor required and Zwift Power for official results.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

eventSubgroups.name: Mountain Massif's Mountain Goat Chase Race (A)
Mountain Massif's Mountain Goat Chase Race (B)
Mountain Massif's Mountain Goat Chase Race (C)
Mountain Massif's Mountain Goat Chase Race (D)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!

name: Rouleur Collective "Hold on!" Race
description: All categories start together in our medium length races. The RC challenges you to hold on to higher categories for a long as you can and push your limits! Hold on, maybe win your own category or perish while trying!

name: Liv Velocity Social Ride
description:
Winter is coming — and the colder days have arrived!
Every Thursday at 19:15 (CEST) we ride the Liv Velocity Social Ride on Zwift.

Join us for a fun and friendly ladies-only social ride and connect with enthusiastic female cyclists from all around the world.
Whether you’re joining to unwind, to get stronger, or simply to enjoy a good chat and a laugh, you’re more than welcome!

This ride is also part of the Velocity Winter WheelsChallenge, so the more kilometers you ride each week, the better it is for the whole group.
Let’s keep those wheels turning together!
First-time group riders are warmly welcomed!
Stronger riders are, as always, invited to help out at the back. 😉
Late joiners are welcome up to 30 minutes into the ride.

Let’s make these winter weeks fun, cozy, and full of great miles together! 🚴‍♀️✨

-- After the Liv Velocity Social Ride you will unlock our beautiful Liv Racing Xstra kit #jerseyunlock --

Riders will be assigned the Liv Langma SL Advanced Disk when joining the pen.
The Liv Langma is a feather-light racing bike delivering speed and strength for any climb, race or ride.

Check our website:
http://www.velocityladies.nl

We look forward to ride with you.

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER?

name: GTN Kit Unlock Ride
description: You asked, and we heard you! Time to unlock that much sought-after kit as worn by Mark, Heather, James... and now you! This is a casual 30 minute group ride and all you need to do is ride at your own pace and finish the event to unlock the GTN jersey - easy!

name: UK Armed Forces Cycling Community Race Night
description:
We are back being "in the chop" is an Aussie expression that you won't read about in the UCI rulebook. It's one of the many nuances of bike racing that only comes with experience.

Sometimes when you're in a race and find yourself in the final break-away bunch you might get asked "are we in the chop?" or "are we chopping it?". This is referring to collusion between the lead riders to work together until the finish. Many times a break-away will be doomed in the final kilometres because everyone starts playing cat and mouse, attacking, sitting up, etc.

But all that goes out the window in the last lap. This is generally where the group comes together and the final push begins. Working together goes out the window and is every man or woman for themselves for the final sprint to the line.

The Chop is a flat fast time-based handicap race. Do you always get blown out the back on the start of a multi grade race trying to hang onto the guy going with A grade pushing 5w/kg. Well not on The Chop. Released in Grades there are time splits from D to A with the intention of all coming together at the end for the big finish. Each Grade wears a different Zwift kit making each grade easily identifiable on who's wheel you want to catch if they go past.

Races happen on Tuesday evenings. We plan to add further races in the future. No single race has been the same. Many variables are at play on The Chop including course (which is generally a flat course), race numbers, and the inevitable sandbaggers. It has been called a team time trail and cat and mouse but the real racers know it only as "THE CHOP". Do you have what it takes to take the glory?


UKAFCC @UKAFCycling
https://www.facebook.com/ukafcycling/

Royal Navy Royal Marines Cycling Association @RNRMCA
https://www.facebook.com/RNRMCA/

Army Cycling @armycycling
https://www.facebook.com/armycycling/

RAF Cycling Association @RAFCycling
https://www.facebook.com/RAFCycling

name: Endura Renegades
description:
Time for some gravel. On the only Zwift course that currently offers any advantage to gravel bikes, we head to the Makuri Islands for the Chain Chomper circuit. Of course, it’s up to you if you still want to go for an MTB or Road bike anyway, that’s your call, but in celebration of our GV500 Gravel Collection, we’ll run it out on the gravel steeds. It’s 2 laps (about 28km), pace will be 2.6-3.0 w/kg depending how the blob is feeling. Not a race, but enough to get the heart rate up before breakfast, lunch or dinner wherever in the world you are.
#kitunlock

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: IOM Endurance Group Ride
description:
Join the Isle of Mans Zwift social group ride in this 60 minute none drop endurance style team ride, with an average pace of 2.0 - 3w/kg. Everyone is welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly supportive atmosphere. The leader paces this ride at around 20-24mph on the flats and efforts will steadily increase to 2.5-3w/kg on the climbs

The Jersey will be auto-selected by adding (MANX) after your name. No TT bikes please, and finally please respect the pace set by the leader, no riding off the front, this is a group ride not a race.
2-3W/kg 60 Min.

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: PACK Run: Social
description:
New to running, coming back after some time off, or simply want a nice warm up or cool down session? Join us as we spend 30 minutes moving the legs in this slow-paced, social event. The goal of the event is to encourage folks to move. 

The leader will average 8kph (7:30min/km) // 5mph (12min/mile) and we encourage runners to go at their own pace if the average pace is too fast and/or use a run/walk strategy.

FULL DESCRIPTION

New to running, coming back after some time off, or simply want a nice warm up or cool down session? Join us as we spend 30 minutes moving the legs in this slow-paced, social event. The goal of the event is to encourage folks to move. 

The leader will average 8kph (7:30min/km) // 5mph (12min/mile) for the duration and it is expected everyone will stay together. This is a timed event, so we encourage runners to go at their own pace if the average pace is too fast and/or use a run/walk strategy.

**Note: if you utilize a run/walk strategy, please start with the walking portion so that you are not overrunning the group on each interval.**

Put your black and orange tops and socks on and let's get moving!

https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-pack-runners

name: 3R Watopia Rally Steady Ride [~3w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

#3r

name: RollCo: The Ascent
description:
⛰️ Your ascent. Rise together.

What is The Ascent?
The Ascent is Rolling Collective’s weekly climbing-focused ride, built around shared effort and individual pacing. It’s a space to work through long climbs alongside others, drawing motivation from the group while riding your own line to the top.

The Ride:
Each week focuses on sustained climbing and elevation. Every other week, The Ascent takes on Alpe du Zwift, giving riders the opportunity to work toward the 25× Alpe Climb achievement as part of a regular, supported routine.

Pacing & Expectations:
Riders are encouraged to climb at their own pace, managing effort and intensity individually. Expect company on the road, encouragement in the chat, and the shared satisfaction of finishing together, wherever that may be on the climb. If you’re looking for a pacer, they can often be found on our discord or club chat.

Why Join Rolling Collective?
All RollCo events are open for any rider, club or non-club, we don’t care who you ride for, just that you’re here for the roll! We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: Team RWB Thursday Social Spin
description:
Join Team RWB, Ireland’s largest eRacing team, every Thursday night at 6:30 PM for a fun and
easy-paced 1-hour spin! We’ll be cruising at a steady 2.0 W/Kg, making it the perfect pace for riders of all levels to relax, chat, and enjoy a social ride together. Whether you’re new to Zwift or a seasoned rider, this is your chance to connect with fellow members of Ireland’s biggest and most passionate Zwift community. So grab your bike, hop on, and let’s make it a great ride together!
🇮🇪🚴‍♂️🚴‍♀️

eventSubgroups.name: Team RWB Thursday Social Spin (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
Our friendly club spin aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience . It will mostly have a group leader and sweeper, will be D and average 1.8-2.2 w/kg with distances ranging 50-60km. To keep the average we'll need riders on the front pushing 2.3-2.3 w/kg and capable sweepers to bring the group back together.

Our ride is zone 2 work we focus on health and well being with this ride zone 2 is the best tool we can use to achieve metabolic health and longevity low heart rate training has numerous of scientifically proven health benefits and here are at RWB we want to provide you with the best form of training.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: The Velos Klick - 1km Challenge
description: A klick is a military term for a distance of 1km, and so Team Velos brings you a challenge to complete 1km in the fastest time possible. We will change courses monthly (all Flat courses) and we will have winners from each category at the end of the month. Choose your bike very carefully as it could make all the difference, the challenge is available every night during the week use it as a warm up for a race or just use it to improve your 1km time or power figures, whatever just have fun.

 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category 
  
 #wtrl #wtrlttt

name: ICE ZWTB
description:
Held in conjunction with our guest zoom conversations (links via ICE app) featuring leading athletes and business leaders, this ride brings together ICE members with the broader Zwift community to share some time in the saddle.
To cater for a range of abilities, Friday Social format is as follows: 10min @ <2wkg // 5min @ +0.5wkg // 30min @ +0.2wkg // 5min @ +0.3wkg // 10min <2wkg

For more about ICE and membership options: internationalcyclingexecutives.com

name: JETT Base Ride (2.7~4.5w/kg)
description:
Welcome to Japan eSports Tempting Team BASE Ride !
 Power :
A group- ave 4.0w/kg～4.5w/kg  
B group- ave 3.3w/kg～3.9w/kg  
C group- ave.2.7w/kg～3.2w/kg
Of course, ZWIFTer of all countries can participate. Riders who are hard at the above power should try grouping by using drafting well. 
If you leave the group behind, please offer with "HELP !!" and ZWIFT CAHT. Sweeper goes for support. Sweeper will not go for support unless he offers "HELP !!". Also, please offer within 10 seconds of time difference as much as possible.

Rule of this group ride 
· You always keep grouping in mind( this is not a race )
· Do not Lead from Leader - yellow beacon 
· Power Ups allowed ( do not use final Sprint )
· TT Bike allowed 
· Sprint is the last lap. (Do not sprint other than the last lap) 
Durable base training of about 110 minutes. Follow the leader's instructions, let's work together and make the best group, the best workout!

Japan eSports Temtping Team BASE RIDE !
A group- ave 4.0w/kg～4.5w/kg  
B group- ave 3.3w/kg～3.9w/kg  
C group- ave.2.7w/kg～3.2w/kg
JETT Base Ride! もちろん、全ての国のZWIFTerが参加できます。上記のパワーでは辛いというライダーはドラフティングをうまく活用してグルーピングにトライして下さい。 
もし、集団から後方に離れてしまった場合、「HELP!!」とZWIFT CAHTで申し出てください。Sweeperが支援に向かいます。「HELP!!」の申し出がない限り、Sweeperは支援には行きません。また、可能な限りタイム差１０秒以内に申し出をお願いします。 
 このグループライドのルール
・常にグルーピングを心がけること(レースではありません）
・リーダーの黄色いビーコンよりも前に出ないこと（２０m以上） 
・Power Ups OK（最終スプリントは使用禁止） 
・TT Bike OK 
・スプリントはしないこと（最後の周のみ実施） 
約100~115分のベーストレーニング、リーダー指示に従い、みんなで協力して最高のグループ、最高のワークアウトにしましょう！ 

#jett #japan

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Stage 2 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 23-29

Keep the prize wheels and bike wheels spinning as we move it over to Mayan San Remo for Stage 2.

name: Ride with Rouleur
description:
The weekly ride with the Rouleur Team. Each week we'll take on a 75km loop with the aim of completeing it as close to 2 hrs as we can.  Top marks for those who cross the line at exactly 2 hrs.  For most riders that will mean riding at between 2.5w/kg and 3 w/kg.  Stay as a group and it will be much easier.

Look out for notifications on our Instagram @rouleurmagazine for information about the ride!  Join the Rouleur club on Zwift.

name: Profitterolls p/b AHDR
description:
Welcome to Profitterolls! This isn't just a ride; it's a fun and faster (C-Pace) format designed to help you improve your fitness, speed, and communication skills. Each week, we'll choose from exciting formats like:

🔴 Wattbomb KOMs
🟠 Progressive Pace
🟢 Confetti Sprints

Both Yellow & Red Beacons are central to the action. An easier option is usually available with one of our beacons if you prefer a more stable ride to enjoy the lively atmosphere. You can join or rejoin this event for up to 30 minutes.

name: GGCC Glasgow Green Cycle Club Thursday Training Ride
description:
GGCC group rides are TRAINING events, category C, with a yellow beacon leader. The beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average wherever possible.

Please look out for our training rides 3 days a week, every week - Tuesdays at 20.45 UTC, Thursdays at 21.00 UTC and Saturdays at 11.30am UTC. Use #ggcc to search, as in https://zwift.com/events/tag/ggcc

The Glasgow Green Cycle Club (GGCC) training rides are intended to provide a challenge for riders, and are open to all timezones. You should ride as a group, following the instructions of the beacon ride leader(s) for any sprints, intervals and spicy segments, regrouping after each when requested. All our group rides also have a closing minirace, to start from behind the beacon.

GGCC is a Glasgow based cycling club whose “in real life” (irl) club runs and training rides are always organized into pace groups, which match the Zwift categories quite well. GGCC also has a good race pedigree and presence in Scotland, and so we have organized this training ride series to suit what we believe is relevant to bike riders’ interests in performance and improvement in our club, in other Glasgow clubs, in Scotland and the UK. Racers will, no doubt, aspire to try our other events, both our Monday 20.10 UTC Zwift races, and our Friday 19.45 UTC TTs.

As we expect our club’s riders to be able to ride 50 miles at a 15mph average on our Sunday rides, such steady riding members will be comfortable with Zwift Category C, and in our various rides we cater for all abilities, through the Zwift ABCD levels. Beacon leaders communicate via Zwift messaging (always) and/or Discord (sometimes) to vary the pace to allow for sprints, intervals, and optionally a mini-race towards the end of the rides, with regrouping after each segment. Make as much use of drafting as you can, for more effective sprinting and intervals.

Results can be seen at the end of the ride on screen, and we will, in common with many other ride series, publish your results on zwiftpower.com too, based on each category.

All bikes and PUs are allowed, but better not to use TT bikes (which don’t draft) on our group rides and races. Please add GGCC (X) to your Zwift name to allow your ride presence to be recognized, where X is your desired ride category. Categories are related to your Functional Threshold Power (FTP); use your FTP and then divide by your weight in kg, then pick the correct ride and group.

C: 2 W/kg to 3 W/kg FTP; the beacon leader will ride to 2.5w/kg pace average.

PLEASE NOTE: Strava data for our rides must be open (not private or hidden). Please use a heart monitor so that we can monitor correlated power and heart rate to help assure fair riding.
You can use our Discord channel to talk to each other at https://discord.gg/UDhdEQA
Please see the GGCC Zwift page link at http://www.glasgowgreencycleclub.co.uk/ for more.

For more about specific Zwift courses for our events, see the Zwift Companion schedule, and also https://zwiftinsider.com/ where there is a wealth of information about all things Zwift.

#ggcc

eventSubgroups.name: GGCC Glasgow Green Cycle Club Thursday Training Ride (C)

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: Zwift Women's Training Club Workout
description:
Welcome to the Zwift Women’s Training Club workouts! 

ZWTC is your community for fun and fitness. Our weekly workouts will take you through some of Zwift’s most popular training plans, and with the flexibility to join in any time, regardless of where we are in the plan! Get the most out of your Zwifting with training designed by world-class coaches, like 3x Olympic Gold medalist, Kristin Armstrong!

The group workouts will be held every 3 hours UTC on Tuesdays and Thursdays. We’ll also offer recovery rides every 3 hours on Wednesdays - be on the look out for special guests joining those! Our main goal is to provide you with a consistent experience week in and week out, to challenge yourself in a supportive group setting, making friends and getting fit along the way! 

Join the Zwift Women’s Training Club to connect with new ride friends, ask questions, and get advice from experts.  https://www.zwift.com/clubs/ZwiftWTC/join

Love these workouts? Check out the club for more opportunities to train with us! We offer social rides every 3 hours on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays in addition to the Tuesday/Thursday workouts.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

eventSubgroups.name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel (A)
Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel (B)

Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel (E)

(profile.playerType == "BROADCASTER") ||
(subgroup.label == 1 && scoring.current >= 390 && scoring.current <= 650) ||
(subgroup.label == 2 && scoring.current >= 270 && scoring.current < 510) ||
(subgroup.label == 3 && scoring.current >= 160 && scoring.current < 390) ||
(subgroup.label == 4 && scoring.current < 270) ||
(subgroup.label == 5 && scoring.current < 160)

name: ZSUN vFUN Social Ride - 1.7-1.9 w/kg
description:
Join ZSUN for our weekly vFUN Social Ride – a relaxed, supportive event perfect for spinning into the weekend. This steady-paced ride (1.7–1.9 w/kg) is all about good vibes, easy chat, and enjoying the ride with fellow Zwifters.

☀️
Expect laughs, friendships, and plenty of encouragement. The yellow beacon will hold to the advertised pace, no matter the course. Wear the iconic #redshorts and feel part of the team!

🗓️
Weekly | Friendly chat encouraged | Everyone welcome

🎧
Join Discord to chat or just listen in

🎁
Complete the ride to earn your ZSUN kit

Put on your favourite playlist, spin those legs, and celebrate cycling in a fun, inclusive space.

eventSubgroups.name: ZSUN vFUN Social Ride - 1.7-1.9 w/kg (D)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a weekly, social event hosted by ZSUN.
☀️ 🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♂️🚴🏼‍♂️🚴🏼🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️🚴🏼🚴🏼🚴🏼‍♀️ 🦄

Join us for a supportive group ride where you will get to wear our legendary #redshorts. Everything is easier with friends and the distraction of easy chatting! Yellow beacon will ride at advertised pace, so don’t ever let the course put you off. The main goal is to have a laugh and make new friends from all over the world. BTW, this is a great way to get ready for your weekend!

Put your music on (and your fan), spin those pedals and celebrate being on your bike in a safe and happy space! Everyone is welcome ☀️

name: Black Sheep Black Friday Workout
description:
The BLACK FRIDAY WORKOUT

It’s always time for a workout ride somewhere! So we are flipping Black Friday on its head. We are Aussies, after all. The Black Friday will be a morning workout for locals. A night before workout for Euro’s and somewhere in between for our friends in America.

Black Friday Workout is a pure social workout at its best. We usually end with a beer, so let’s make it a coffee. And hey, why don’t we throw in a new kit as well. Finish the workout to unlock your very own Black Sheep Racing Club kit in-game.

Join us for the Black Friday Workout each Friday at 6:30 am AEST.

The race lap will be with the yellow beacon, who will win the cup ?

If you want to help as sweeps to hold the group together, you can tell us and we will look into helping you to help the Team

Every Friday of Australia Oceania Morning

Those who have never been here always Welcome to join our great Team. We ‘re waiting for you all

On Behalf of the Team, Thank you

name: WTRL Team Time Trial - Zone 36
description:
WTRL Team Time Trial #365

Our weekly WTRL Team Time Trial (TTT) allows teams of 3-8 Zwifters to race against the clock together throughout the day to achieve the fastest possible times. Teams are arranged into WTRL's unique Coffee Classes and feature both women's only Coffee Classes and mixed gender too.
  
 There are 30 racing events and teams can be registered up 30 minutes before each race event starts at www.wtrl.racing/ttt-home/ and you can pick your start time from the available slots. Each team is issued a unique RacePass that must be clicked by each team member to gain access to the event for sign-up. You'll also be able to check out the rules, course records and results which update live throughout the day.
  
 Teams & Riders must be registered with www.WTRL.racing and you'll need to be sharing data, via your Zwift Profile Connections with www.zwiftpower.com.
  
 If you are looking for a team, head to our dedicated WTRL TTT Facebook page where plenty of teams are always happy to welcome guest riders and new team mates (https://www.facebook.com/groups/WTRLTTT). Not on Facebook? Feel free to drop us an email with your preferred WTRL TTT Zone and current Zwift Category

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Restart Esports - Thursday Thumper
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

eventSubgroups.name: Restart Esports - Thursday Thumper (A)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join in on a new weekly race: the Restart Racing Thursday Thumper.

Restart Racing is an eRacing team that likes to race hard and have fun. eSports gives us an amazing platform to better ourselves as bike racers and is a great way to make friends and enjoy ourselves. 

Each week will feature a different course ranging in distance from 45-60kms with a target finish time around 1.5hrs. Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.

Cats A & B riders require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) plus a heart rate monitor. Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual record and make analysis 'Public' on Zwift Power while using a direct drive trainer and power meter.
Cat C will require power source (power meter or smart trainer) and heart rate monitor for podium spots.

Cat A - 4.0+ (min 250W)
Cat B - 3.2 - 4.0 w/kg (min 200W)
Cat C - 2.5 - 3.2 w/kg (min 150W)
Cat D - 1.0 - 2.5 w/kg

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Rhino Racing Workout Thursday
description:
This plan will peak end of April 2026 - last 4 weeks. We will break for the Spring/Summer and back in the Fall.
March 26 - 2 x 10min max effort
April 2 - 3 x 10min sweet Spot
April 9 - 3 x 8min sweet spot
April 16 - Recover 1hr z2

Thank you for joining us. This is the last ride before breaking for the summer. It will restart Fall 2026. See you Soon!

Each week we will have a Ride Leader (Yellow beacon) leading a steady, structured endurance builder focused on improving muscular stamina and threshold power. Following a 4 week cycle, 3 week build and 1 week off. This is a banded group ride. We’re in base training phases through the winter, laying the foundation for fitness before moving into build phase as we head into the new year. There will be Pair this with ZRL racing each week, and you’re set. It’s all about consistency. There will be cues and tips throughout the ride to help you manage the effort and stay in the right zones.

These workouts come straight from CoachCat’s training library, where you can gain access to over 600 workouts and 100 training plans during a 30-day FREE Trial on FasCatCoaching.com. Come join the suffering while unlocking new routes.

name: Move for Lex
description:
The Move for Lex workout honors avid Zwifter and cyclist Alexa Leary, who was seriously injured in a bike crash in July 2021. 

Lex was one of Australia’s top emerging junior triathletes at the time of her accident and is now facing months of rehabilitation. 

The #moveforlex movement is all about moving for those who can't and we'd love to see a huge group moving for Lex here every Friday for a workout.

For more information on the Move For Lex movement visit https://www.moveforlex.com/

name: AATC Thursday Interval Workout
description:
WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Dana of the Ann Arbor Triathlon Club - Group workouts make training so much more fun. The Ann Arbor Triathlon Club promotes the sport of triathlon in Southeast Michigan. We have all levels of Triathletes.  

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop.

HELPFUL HINT

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. 

For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift
